meta.description: On-demand branded COMIC BOOK generator on any topic. Trigger manually (form: topic/brief, title, art style — cartoon/anime/realistic/etc., number of scenes, optional per-scene descriptions, audience) or programmatically (On Command). AI writes a script with locked character & place designs; the workflow generates a reference ANCHOR image per character and place, then renders each panel via the image provider /images/edits conditioned on those anchors (with a /images/generations fallback) so characters, places and style stay CONSISTENT across panels. Render lays out a branded comic page (caption bars + speech bubbles); Gotenberg makes the PDF; a pure-JS ZIP bundles the panel PNGs, anchor images and script. Writes to /reports/awareness/comics. Graceful placeholders if no image key. Anchor and panel images are generated in parallel (COMIC_CONCURRENCY, default 2) with a fail-fast per-call timeout (IMAGE_TIMEOUT, default 90s) at a chosen image quality (form, or COMIC_IMAGE_QUALITY; default medium). Images are written to a temp work dir on disk and only file paths flow through the workflow (never base64), and the rendered HTML is passed to Gotenberg as binary — so n8n execution data stays tiny and the editor/process never choke on inline images.

parameters.formDescription: Pick a topic, art style and number of scenes (optionally describe each scene). Creates character & place anchor images, then a branded, consistent comic (PDF + image bundle).

values.placeholder: Any topic, e.g. spotting phishing emails at work
Optional — one scene per line. Leave blank to let the AI write the story.

parameters.jsonBody:
={
  "model": "{{ $env.OLLAMA_DEFAULT_MODEL || 'llama3.1:8b' }}",
  "stream": false,
  "keep_alive": "10m",
  "options": { "temperature": 0.6, "num_predict": 8000 },
  "messages": [ {"role":"system","content":"You are a comic-book writer and character designer. For the given topic, audience, art style and panel count (and optional scene beats), produce a SINGLE valid JSON object that will drive image generation, so VISUAL CONSISTENCY is essential. Keys: title (short comic title); logline (one sentence); characters (1-4 items, each {id (a short lowercase slug), name, role, visual (a DETAILED, LOCKED appearance description — approximate age, build, hair, skin tone, exact clothing and colours, and 1-2 distinguishing features; this exact description will be reused for every panel, so make it specific and keep it identical in spirit everywhere)}); places (1-3 items, each {id (slug), name, visual (a detailed, locked description of the setting, lighting and mood)}); panels (an array containing EXACTLY the requested number of panel objects — never fewer; if you cannot fit detail, keep each panel concise but still output the full count, each {scene (the beat), place (a place id), characters (array of character ids present, may be empty), shot (one of: wide, medium, close-up), action (a vivid one-sentence description of what is happening, for an illustrator), caption (optional short narration, <=14 words), dialogue (0-2 items, each {speaker (a character id), text (<=12 words)})}); takeaways (3-4 short, plain-English security-awareness lessons the reader should remember from this story — each one sentence, actionable, written for the audience). Tell a clear, engaging story appropriate to the audience that lands the security-awareness message. Reference only character/place ids that you defined. Do NOT describe any on-image text, captions, speech bubbles, logos or UI — those are added later in layout. Do NOT use markdown code fences. Output ONLY the JSON object."}, {"role":"user","content": {{ JSON.stringify('Create a comic. Topic/brief: ' + ($json.topic||'staying safe online') + '. Audience: ' + ($json.audience||'General audience') + '. Art style: ' + ($json.style||'Cartoon') + '. The "panels" array MUST contain EXACTLY ' + ($json.numScenes||6) + ' panel objects — never fewer, never more. Count them before replying.' + (($json.scenes && $json.scenes.length) ? (' Use these scene beats in order, one per panel: ' + $json.scenes.map(function(s,k){return (k+1)+') '+s;}).join('  ')) : ' Invent a clear, engaging story.') + ($json.title ? (' Suggested title: ' + $json.title + '.') : '')) }} } ]
}
